verses.text: O Cronista resume em vinte e três versículos o longo relato do cerco contra Jerusalém (cp. **2Rs 18.17-19.37** ; **Is 36-38** ). A resposta de Deus ao ataque do rei Senaqueribe da Assíria foi uma bênção que resultou da fidelidade de Judá e Ezequias em buscar o Senhor.\n\n

O Cronista resume em vinte e três versículos o longo relato do cerco contra Jerusalém (cp. **2Rs 18.17-19.37** ; **Is 36-38** ). A resposta de Deus ao ataque do rei Senaqueribe da Assíria foi uma bênção que resultou da fidelidade de Judá e Ezequias em buscar o Senhor.\n\n

O ataque de Senaqueribe não foi uma surpresa; Ezequias o provocou ao romper um tratado ( **2Rs 18.7** , **20** ). Como a água era crucial tanto para o atacante quanto para o defensor na guerra de cerco, Ezequias garantiu que o exército de Senaqueribe não tivesse acesso ao suprimento de água da cidade. Em uma das façanhas de engenharia mais famosas dos tempos antigos, o túnel de Ezequias conectou as águas frescas da Fonte de Giom ao tanque de Siloé, tornando a água acessível de dentro das muralhas da cidade. Este famoso túnel tem quase 530 metros de comprimento e cerca de dois metros de altura. A famosa inscrição de Ezequias, descoberta no túnel em 1880, descreve como os escavadores se encontraram para unir as duas extremidades. Embora grande parte do túnel consistisse em aquedutos subterrâneos naturais, foi uma estratégia tremenda para proteger os cidadãos da cidade.

32:6 he . . . gathered them together . . . in the street—that is, the large open space at the gate of Eastern cities. Having equipped his soldiers with a full suit of military accoutrements, he addressed them in an animated strain, dwelling on the motives they had to inspire courage and confidence of success, especially on their consciousness of the favor and helping power of God.

32:7 There be more with us than with him - We have more power than they have. (These words he quotes from the prophet Elisha, Kg2 6:16). This was soon proved to be true by the slaughter made by the angel of the Lord in the Assyrian camp.

32:8 After this did Sennacherib king of Assyria send his servants to Jerusalem,.... Who are mentioned by name, Kg2 18:17 this was after Hezekiah had given him a large quantity of silver and gold to depart, and he did depart from him, Kg2 18:14 but he himself laid siege against Lachish, and all his power with him; one of the cities of Judah, see Isa 36:2, from hence he dispatched them unto Hezekiah king of Judah, and unto all Judah that were at Jerusalem;

32:9 After this did Sennacherib - Having received the silver and gold mentioned above, he withdrew his army, but shortly after he sent Rab-shakeh with a blasphemous message. This is the fact mentioned here.

32:20 In those days Hezekiah was sick,.... Of which sickness, and of his prayer, and of the sign given him, see Isa 38:1 and the notes there. and the notes there. 2 Chronicles 32:25 ch2 32:25 ch2 32:25 ch2 32:25But Hezekiah rendered not again according to the benefit done unto him,.... Both in the deliverance of him and his people from the king of Assyria, and the recovery of him from his sickness: for his heart was lifted up; with pride, because of the wonderful defeat of the Assyrian army in his favour, the miracle wrought at his recovery from illness, the riches and honour conferred upon him, the presents brought him from his neighbours, and especially the embassy of the king of Babylon to him: therefore there was wrath upon…
O destino de Senaqueribe é relatado de forma semelhante ao relato em Reis (veja **2Rs 19.35-37** ).
A conclusão do Cronista oferece um final adequado para a história: o desastre para a Assíria resultou em paz doméstica para Judá, e tanto o Senhor quanto Ezequias receberam honra de outras nações.
A conclusão do Cronista oferece um final adequado para a história: o desastre para a Assíria resultou em paz doméstica para Judá, e tanto o Senhor quanto Ezequias receberam honra de outras nações.

32:24 Moreover, he provided him cities,.... Where he had the above storehouses and stalls, and convenient dwellings for those that looked after them, and were over his cattle, small and great, as follows; the Vulgate Latin version reads six cities in some copies (b): and possessions of flocks and herds in abundance; in which the riches of men, and even of kings, lay in those times: for God had given him substance very much; for all was owing to his disposing providence, let it come which way it might.
32:25 Hezekiah rendered not again - He got into a vain confidence, took pleasure in his riches, and vainly showed them to the messengers of the king of Babylon. See on Kg2 20:12 (note), etc.
O humilde arrependimento de Ezequias e do povo agradou a Deus, então a ira do Senhor não caiu sobre eles durante a vida de Ezequias.
A riqueza de Ezequias o destacou como um rei semelhante a Salomão.
A riqueza de Ezequias o destacou como um rei semelhante a Salomão.

A riqueza de Ezequias o destacou como um rei semelhante a Salomão.
Os detalhes da visita dos enviados babilônicos estão registrados de forma mais completa em **2Rs 20.12-20** . O Cronista fornece um comentário sobre os propósitos de Deus nessa visita.
32:32 The vision of Isaiah - See this prophet, 2 Chronicles 36-39.

32:33 Chiefest of the sepulchres - This respect they paid to him who, since David, had been the best of all their kings. I shall subjoin a few things from the Targum on this chapter. 2 Chr 32:1. "After these things which Hezekiah did, and their establishment, the Lord appointed by his Word to bring Sennacherib, king of Assyria, and his army, into the land of Israel, that he might destroy the Assyrians in the land of the house of Judah, and smite their troops on the mountains of Jerusalem, and deliver all their spoils into the hands of Hezekiah and his people: wherefore Sennacherib came with immense armies, which could not be numbered;
